goal: Fix Aether infrastructure gaps: wisdom pipeline conversion failure, state-mutate bracket notation bug, and silent command failure logging

phases.description: state-write uses cobra.NoArgs but callers (init.md, plan.md) pass jq output as a positional argument. The command silently rejects these args. Fix by accepting an optional positional JSON blob that replaces the full state file.
phases.name: Fix state-write positional arg rejection
success_criteria: state-write accepts a positional JSON argument and writes it to COLONY_STATE.json
state-write --field/--value mode still works (backward compatible)
All existing state_extra_test.go tests pass

constraints: Must call state-write with a positional JSON argument
Must assert the command currently fails due to cobra.NoArgs
tasks.goal: Write a failing test proving state-write rejects positional JSON arguments
hints: Follow existing TestStateWrite pattern: saveGlobals, resetRootCmd, newTestStore, parseEnvelope
success_criteria: Test fails (RED) because cobra.NoArgs rejects the positional argument
go test ./cmd/ -run TestStateWrite -v shows failure

constraints: Must preserve existing --field/--value flag behavior as primary interface
When positional arg provided, parse as JSON and write directly to COLONY_STATE.json
Change Args from cobra.NoArgs to cobra.MaximumNArgs(1)
tasks.goal: Fix state-write to accept a positional JSON argument for backward compatibility with shell-script callers
hints: If len(args) > 0, validate args[0] as JSON, save via store.SaveJSON
If both positional and flags provided, return an error
success_criteria: Test from 1.1 now passes (GREEN)
Existing TestStateWrite for --field/--value still passes
init.md and plan.md callers succeed with positional JSON

phases.description: pheromone-write already computes SHA-256 content_hash and PheromoneSignal already has ReinforcementCount. But the write always appends a new signal instead of checking for existing signals with same type + content_hash. Implement dedup: match on type + content_hash, increment ReinforcementCount on duplicate.
phases.name: Implement pheromone content dedup with reinforcement
success_criteria: Duplicate pheromone-write calls produce 1 signal with ReinforcementCount incremented
Non-duplicate calls still append new signals
Output envelope reports replaced:true for dedup cases

constraints: Must call pheromone-write twice with same type and content
Must assert only 1 signal exists after two writes
tasks.goal: Write a failing test proving pheromone-write creates duplicate signals instead of reinforcing
hints: Create test store with empty pheromones.json
success_criteria: Test fails (RED) because pheromone-write currently appends duplicates
go test ./pkg/colony/ -run TestPheromoneDedup -v shows failure

constraints: Check must happen after contentHash computation and before appending
Only match on active signals (Active == true)
On match: increment ReinforcementCount, max Strength, update timestamp, return replaced:true
tasks.goal: Add dedup logic to pheromone_write.go: scan existing signals for matching type + content_hash, increment ReinforcementCount instead of appending
hints: PheromoneSignal already has ReinforcementCount *int field with omitempty
Initialize to 1 if nil, then increment on each duplicate
success_criteria: Test from 2.1 now passes (GREEN)
Non-duplicate calls still append new signals
Output envelope correctly reports replaced:true for dedup cases

phases.description: state-mutate's reFieldSet regex uses [\w.]+ which does not match bracket notation like 'plan.phases[0].status'. When callers use bracket paths, the expression is rejected. Fix by widening the regex and relying on sjson's native bracket path support.
phases.name: Fix state-mutate bracket notation in expression parser
success_criteria: state-mutate accepts bracket notation paths like '.plan.phases[0].status = "completed"'
Existing dot-notation paths still work
sjson correctly resolves bracket notation

constraints: Must create COLONY_STATE.json with plan.phases containing at least 1 phase
tasks.goal: Write a failing test proving state-mutate rejects bracket notation paths
hints: The regex currently only accepts [\w.]+ for the field path
success_criteria: Test fails (RED) because reFieldSet regex rejects bracket notation
go test ./cmd/ -run TestStateMutateBracket -v shows failure

constraints: sjson.SetRawBytes already handles bracket notation natively — no other changes needed
tasks.goal: Update reFieldSet regex to accept bracket notation paths in state_cmds.go
hints: sjson supports paths like 'plan.phases.0.status' or 'plan.phases.[0].status' natively
Verify existing dot-notation tests still pass after regex change
success_criteria: Test from 3.1 now passes (GREEN)
Existing dot-notation state-mutate tests still pass
go test ./cmd/ -run TestStateMutate -v shows all green

phases.description: worktree_merge.go and suggest.go are dead code (no callers from any colony command). Deprecate using existing deprecated_cmds.go pattern. Add error logging to silent error sites in active code paths: rotateSpawnTree, handleObserveEvent, parseMetadata, and low-risk os.* calls.
phases.name: Deprecate dead code and add silent error logging
success_criteria: Dead code formally deprecated with clear JSON response
All active silent error sites produce log output on failure
No control flow changes — errors remain non-fatal

constraints: Verify all 5 suggest subcommands (analyze, approve, list, reject, clear) are dead code before deprecating
tasks.goal: Replace worktree_merge.go and suggest.go implementations with deprecated command stubs
hints: deprecated_cmds.go shows the pattern: register command, set Deprecated/Annotations, silence usage/errors
success_criteria: go build ./cmd/aether compiles
worktree-merge and suggest-* commands return deprecated JSON response
go test ./cmd/... passes (dead code tests removed or kept minimal)

constraints: maintenance.go:156, install_cmd.go:199,297, setup_cmd.go:207: unchecked os.* calls
Do NOT change control flow — errors remain non-fatal, just logged
tasks.goal: Add log.Printf error logging to all silent error sites in active code paths
hints: Use log.Printf with descriptive prefix for each site
pipeline.go line 120 already has log.Printf — follow that exact style
success_criteria: Each silent error site now logs before discarding
go test ./... passes with no new failures
go vet ./... shows no new issues

phases.description: Run full test suite with race detection and verify all fixes work together without regressions.
phases.name: Integration verification
success_criteria: Full test suite passes with race detection
No regressions in any existing tests
Binary builds cleanly

constraints: Must run go test ./... -race from repo root
tasks.goal: Run full test suite, go vet, and binary build to verify zero regressions
success_criteria: go test ./... -race exits 0 with no failures
go vet ./... exits 0
go build ./cmd/aether succeeds

learnings.claim: cobra.MaximumNArgs(N) with positional arg + flag mutual exclusion is a clean backward-compat pattern for CLI commands
learnings.evidence: state-write changed from NoArgs to MaximumNArgs(1); existing --field/--value tests pass, new positional JSON test passes
